rulings.Case Title: ELIZABETH KARNAZES VS. ALBERT LEE ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion / Discovery calendar for Friday, May 30, 2025, Line 1. 1 - DEFENDANT ALBERT LEE's MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Continued to July 25, 2025 per court order. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: MARTIN FIDEL PENA VS. STATE FARM MUTUAL AUTOMOBILE INSURANCE COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: PLAINTIFF MARTIN PENA'S MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion / Discovery calendar for Friday, May 30, 2025, Line 6. PLAINTIFF MARTIN PENA'S MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION. Plaintiff Martin Pena's motion for reconsideration is treated as an invitation for the Court to exercise its inherent authority to reconsider pre-judgment rulings, the Court accepts that invitation, and upon reconsideration based on the evidence submitted by Mr. Pena on this motion the March 18, 20225 order granting defendant State Farm Mutual Automobile Insurance Company's motion for summary judgment is vacated and will be superseded by a new and different order denying both State Farm's motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication. While it is likely that Mr. Pena has not met the requirements for statutory reconsideration of the order granting State Farm's motion for summary judgment per CCP 1008, the Court exercises its inherent authority to reconsider that order as provided for in Le Francois v. Goel (2005) 35 Cal. 4th 1094 and as discussed at the March 18, 2025 hearing which might be an avenue available to Mr. Pena. The Court does so in the interests of justice because Mr. Pena has now provided evidence in admissible form showing that there are triable disputes regarding both of his causes of action. The evidence submitted by Mr. Pena shows that there are triable issues whether: 1) State Farm properly denied both of Mr. Pena's insurance claims; 2) Mr. Pena made any material misrepresentations to State Farm; 3) Mr. Pena failed to cooperate with State Farm during State Farm's investigation of Mr. Pena's insurance claims; and 4) State Farm acted in bad faith and without a reasonable basis in denying both of Mr. Pena's insurance claims. These triable issues establish that State Farm is not entitled to summary determination in its favor on either of Mr. Pena's two causes of action for breach of contract and breach of the covenant of good faith and fair dealing. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: KIFFEN LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY VS. HERBL, INC, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To File Under Seal Portions Of The Motion For Summary Judgment Or, In The Alternative Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion / Discovery calendar for Friday, May 30, 2025, Line 2. DEFENDANTS MICHAEL BEAUDRY, AND JACQUELINE HARTWELL's Motion To File Under Seal Portions Of The Motion For Summary Judgment Or, In The Alternative Summary Adjudication. Defendants Michael Beaudry and Jacqueline Hartwell's motion to seal portions of motion for summary judgment/adjudication papers is denied. Defendants' only support for this motion is that certain documents have been designated confidential and conclusory statements in an attorney declaration. A sealing order must be supported by a "specific showing" of "facts" as to all the factors in CRC 2.550(d). (McNair v. NCAA (2015) 234 Cal. App. 4th 25, 35). Parties' agreement to designate documents as confidential, much less one side's unilateral decision to do so, is not a sufficient basis for a sealing order. (CRC 2.551(a)). For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication (Motions)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion / Discovery calendar for Friday, May 30, 2025, Line 3. DEFENDANT ARCADIOS PRODUCE, INC.'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication. Defendant Arcadios Produce, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to the first and second causes of action for hostile work environment sexual harassment and failure to prevent harassment and granted as to the third and fourth causes of action for FEHA retaliation and wrongful termination in violation of public policy. rulings.Case Title: LUKASZ FILINSKI VS. AUSSY MANUHU ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion / Discovery calendar for Friday, May 30, 2025, Line 5. DEFENDANT AUSSY MANUHU's DEMURRER to COMPLAINT. Defendant Aussy Manuhu's demurrer to all ten causes of action in the civil complaint filed by plaintiff Lukasz Filinski is sustained without leave to amend to allege any claims in a civil complaint but without prejudice to alleging some or all of the same claims in the parties' pending family law case, Filinksi v. Manuhu FDI-22-797233. Because the primary allegation underlying all of Mr. Filinski's alleged civil claims concerns the characterization and entitlement to property that is also at issue In the family law case, the parties' must litigate those claims in the family law case, not a civil action. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom.
